summary.brief: State v. Coleman. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Defendant and J lived together in Hillsboro. In September 2022, police were dispatched to their residence when J called 9-1-1.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: J told the 9-1-1 operator that defendant had taken her phone and that he had attached three belts around her neck and had tried to lift her up and kill her.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At trial, J did not want to testify against defendant. She testified that she called the police because she was angry and that she sustained injuries when she tripped and fell.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: J told a paramedic that defendant had strangled her with a belt and shoved a remote control into her mouth.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Muncy testified about the physiology of the neck and throat and about how it takes very little pressure or time to strangle a person to death.
evidence_index.speaker: Theresa Muncy

evidence_index.text: The trial court expressed some skepticism about the necessity for the expert testimony, observing that 'they used to kill people by hanging them,' and that it was common knowledge 'that if you lose blood to the brain and airflow to the lungs, you’re going to drop dead.'
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: Muncy explained that it takes very little pressure to compress the jugular vein and the carotid artery.
evidence_index.speaker: Theresa Muncy

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not abuse its discretion in its OEC 403 balancing of the probative value of the evidence against the danger of unfair prejudice.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Here, the testimony had probative value because it provided the jury with information about how easily and quickly a person can sustain injury or death from strangulation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports being strangled with a belt by the defendant.

focused_exam.neck: Signs of strangulation reported but not detailed in judgment

requestables.name: Paramedic Report
requestables.description: Paramedic's assessment of the patient at the scene

reveal.result_summary: Patient reported strangulation with a belt and insertion of a remote control into the mouth.
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates potential airway compromise and need for immediate medical evaluation.
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt immediate medical evaluation and possible imaging.

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with signs of strangulation

decision_points.prompt: What immediate action should be taken?

options.clinical_reasoning: Strangulation can lead to airway compromise; immediate assessment is critical.

options.description: Delay assessment and focus on gathering more history
options.clinical_reasoning: Delaying assessment can lead to worsening of potential airway compromise.

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant focused on gathering more history instead of immediate care

expected_action_court.description: Immediate airway assessment should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established immediate assessment is standard

patient_outcome.description: Patient survived the incident but with potential long-term psychological effects

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to provide immediate care
Causation established - delay in care could have worsened condition

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide immediate airway assessment
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Immediate assessment could have prevented potential complications
